sourceSummary.oneLine: AspenView cybersecurity risk management report excerpt describing implemented risk controls, residual risk acceptability, and post-market cybersecurity management.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports cybersecurity risk management documentation, residual risk assessment, and post-production vulnerability monitoring and patching processes for AspenView software.
keyPoints: Risk mitigation measures include access control, authentication, session restrictions, account lockout, network authentication, encryption, update controls, logging, and security threat detection.
All listed residual risk control items RM-RC-01-01 through RM-RC-13 are evaluated as Acceptable.

Post-market cybersecurity management includes monitoring NIST NVD, CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog, third-party advisories, security testing, patch timelines, disclosure handling, and customer communications.

entities.name: Aspen Imaging Healthcare Inc
entities.evidence: The method of obtaining information after production for Aspen Imaging Healthcare Inc AspenView software is performed according to the measurement, analysis and improvement procedures of the quality management manual

entities.name: Food and Drug Administration
entities.evidence: it shall be reported and necessary safety measures shall be taken as prescribed by the Commissioner of the Food and Drug Administration

entities.name: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) National Vulnerability Database (NVD)
entities.evidence: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) National Vulnerability Database (NVD)

entities.name: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog
entities.evidence: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog

entities.name: Security Individual Contractor (Dong Hyun Kim)
entities.evidence: Security Individual Contractor (Dong Hyun Kim): Overall responsibility for cybersecurity management, responsible for monitoring, identifying, and addressing vulnerabilities with the help of the development team.

concepts.name: Authentication Controls
concepts.evidence: Authentication controls have been significantly strengthened for AspenView by increasing the complexity of password requirement, locking users out after repeated trying to login unsuccessfully, and ensuring that a single account can only have a single session logged into the software.

concepts.name: Authorization Controls
concepts.evidence: Updates to the software can only be made by an administrator, and an admin can only patch the software with a set of files that need to be validated by a password from the manufacturer.

concepts.name: Cryptography Controls
concepts.evidence: All patient data is encrypted using DICOM standards, and credentials are encrypted with SHA-256 method to ensure decryption cannot happen without manufacturer’s decryption methods.

concepts.name: Event Detection & Logging Control
concepts.evidence: Traceability of data handled by AspenView have been significantly improved by collecting information on all login transactions, navigation history within the program, modification and deletion operations on files in AspenView are all recorded and organized on a day-to-day basis

concepts.name: Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure
concepts.evidence: Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure and Related Processes

evidence.claim: The document is titled Cyber Security Risk Management Report for AspenView software, revision 3, dated 2024.09.01.
evidence.evidenceText: Cyber Security Risk Management Report Document No. AspenView-RMF Revision No. 3 Model: AspenView software Revision Date. 2024.09.01

evidence.claim: AspenView implements user identification and authentication using unique account ID/password credentials with differentiated access authority by account.
evidence.evidenceText: User identification and authentication functions are implemented with ID/PW unique to each account. The authority for the information that can be accessed by each account is differentially granted.

evidence.claim: AspenView restricts multiple simultaneous access to the same account using session information.
evidence.evidenceText: AspenView restricts multiple access to the same account by checking the session information of the account. While a given account is logged in, no other session can login with that account

evidence.claim: AspenView restricts account access after more than five incorrect password entries.
evidence.evidenceText: AspenView detects continuous login attempts using an incorrect PW, and restricts access to the account if the PW is incorrectly entered more than 5 times

evidence.claim: AspenView validates network authentication information for data transmission and relies on hospital network policy for on-device network controls.
evidence.evidenceText: AspenView checks and validates network authentication information for data transmission. On-device network follows the strict network policy of hospitals

evidence.claim: AspenView password rules require at least 12 alphanumeric characters and a special character; passwords are encrypted using SHA-256 hash protocol and masked during entry.
evidence.evidenceText: Password rules have been reinforced to require at least 12 alpha-numeric characters and a special character when creating an account. Passwords are encrypted using SHA-256 hash protocol. When entering the password, it is displayed as *** characters.

evidence.claim: AspenView can be configured through Windows OS Firewall to expose and activate only desired ports.
evidence.evidenceText: AspenView can be configured to expose and active only the ports the user desires using Windows OS Firewall

evidence.claim: AspenView encrypts patient information when transmitting DICOM data files and can decrypt uploaded encrypted data.
evidence.evidenceText: AspenView will encrypt patient information when transmitting as DICOM data files, and is able to decrypt the encrypted data that was uploaded

evidence.claim: AspenView can delete stale data after transmitting medical information when configured by the user.
evidence.evidenceText: Upon user configuration, stale data can be deleted after transmitting medical information from AspenView

evidence.claim: Product updates are limited to administrators, who require a manufacturer-provided password to unzip and install new releases.
evidence.evidenceText: Deployment of product updates are limited to only the administrators, and even the admins must have the required password from the manufacturer to be able to unzip and install the new release

evidence.claim: AspenView update files require digital signatures.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital signatures are required on the update files of AspenView

evidence.claim: AspenView records access records, patient information inquiry, data creation, change, and deletion logs on a daily basis.
evidence.evidenceText: When accessing a user's medical device, log information such as access records, patient information inquiry, data creation, change, and deletion are thoroughly recorded by AspenView on daily basis

evidence.claim: AspenView relies on the operating system firewall and anti-virus programs to detect alarms, errors, and delays due to security threats.
evidence.evidenceText: AspenView works with the operating system’s default firewall and anti-virus programs to detect alarms, errors, and delays due to security threats. Counter measures are taken by the on-device anti-virus programs that must be installed along with AspenView

evidence.claim: All residual risk control items shown in the excerpt are evaluated as Acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: RM-RC-01-01 5 accept N N Y Acceptable ... RM-RC-13 5 accept N N Y Acceptable

evidence.claim: Risk management for AspenView software was verified and residual risk was acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: This AspenView software risk management plan was properly implemented, proved that residual risk was acceptable... Risk management for AspenView software has been verified.

evidence.claim: Post-production cybersecurity monitoring includes monthly NIST NVD and CISA checks, weekly third-party software advisory reviews, and quarterly comprehensive security audits and assessments.
evidence.evidenceText: Monthly checks for updates from NIST NVD and CISA Weekly reviews of third-party software updates and advisories Quarterly comprehensive security audits and assessments

evidence.claim: Periodic security testing includes quarterly vulnerability assessments, semi-annual penetration testing, and annual comprehensive security audits.
evidence.evidenceText: Quarterly vulnerability assessments. Semi-annual penetration testing. Annual comprehensive security audits.

evidence.claim: Patch timelines are one week for critical vulnerabilities, two weeks for high-risk vulnerabilities, one month for medium-risk vulnerabilities, and three months for low-risk vulnerabilities.
evidence.evidenceText: Critical Vulnerabilities: Patches developed and released within 1 week. High-Risk Vulnerabilities: Patches developed and released within 2 weeks. Medium-Risk Vulnerabilities: Patches developed and released within 1 month. Low-Risk Vulnerabilities: Patches developed and released within 3 months.

evidence.claim: Coordinated vulnerability disclosure includes public policy availability, a dedicated contact point, acknowledgement within 48 hours, and initial assessment and triage within 240 hours.
evidence.evidenceText: Publicly accessible vulnerability disclosure policy on our website. Dedicated contact point (email and web form) for receiving vulnerability reports. Acknowledgement of reports within 48 hours. Initial assessment and triage within 240 hours.

evidence.claim: Customer remediation communications include advance notifications, release notes, email, customer portals, in-application notifications, technical support, and follow-up communications.
evidence.evidenceText: Advance notifications to customers about forthcoming patches and updates... Detailed release notes accompanying patches and updates... Use of multiple communication channels, including email, customer portals, and in-application notifications... Technical support available... Follow-up communications

reviewFlags.reason: Source text is an excerpt from pages 20-28 of 46; front matter, approvals, signatures, and full document context are not present.

Authority is ambiguous: metadata indicates FDA and the text references the Commissioner of the Food and Drug Administration, but the excerpt does not identify a specific FDA submission, regulation, or jurisdiction beyond that wording.
Product metadata productSignal is HnVUE, while the source text model is AspenView software; relationship between HnVUE and AspenView is not established in the excerpt.
Possible transcription or consistency issue: one mitigation item appears as AX-RC-01-07 while adjacent controls use RM-RC identifiers.
OCR/text quality has minor line-break and wording artifacts, including split table headers and phrases such as 'expose and active only the ports'.
